engine: yazelix envelope/yzx-envelope.sh (flake app yzx-envelope, PR FlexNetOS/yazelix#103)

mount_table.rationale: The private root is an empty tmpfs, not an overlayfs of the host root: the envelope presents ONLY what is declared, so host state can never leak in by omission and nothing the envelope writes can shadow-modify host paths. Unprivileged overlayfs is also blocked by the same host AppArmor userns policy the executor already navigates — tmpfs is both stricter and portable.
The store is the single immutable binary provider (path law); a read-only bind gives zero-copy access with no write channel. At release time the portable closure replaces this bind (I09/I17).

Only name resolution and user identity are bound, individually and read-only. The full host /etc (machine identity, systemd config, apt state) stays invisible — a full /etc bind would re-couple the envelope to the host lifecycle.
A fresh procfs scoped to the private PID namespace: the envelope sees only its own processes (probe pid=2).
A minimal /dev (null, zero, urandom, tty…). Real device nodes (GPU, others) enter only via explicit on-demand dev-binds (I11).
Private scratch per envelope — the multi-session collision boundary proven by ARCHBP-070 (three concurrent envelopes, zero cross-visibility).

The home overlay is an empty tmpfs over $HOME, not an overlayfs: home residuals (~/.claude and peers, the G5 retirement surface) are invisible inside, and nothing written inside can land in the host home. Durable agent state re-enters only through declared durable binds once the T3 migration relocates it.

mount_table.path: /durable/** (caller-declared, e.g. meta/var, postgres datadir, relocated runtime)
mount_table.rationale: Durable state crosses the boundary only by explicit declaration (--durable SRC:DST), sourced from the tier map's durable targets: meta/var/lib/postgresql (canonical PostgreSQL/RuVector macro-state), meta/var/lib/redb, meta/var/xdg-data. Writes persist across envelope exit (ARCHBP-068) — the durable plane, not the envelope, is the persistence boundary (I05/I06).
GPU and device nodes appear only when acquired (--gpu / --device) and vanish with the namespace on exit — the envelope-side hook of the t8 control plane (I11/I12), proven by ARCHBP-069.
